claim_boundary: Host-side NEST diagnostic of heritable neural parameter diversity only. Not mechanism promotion, not a baseline freeze, not hardware/Spynnaker evidence, not public usefulness proof.

code_change.description:
1. Add neural_factor attributes (tau_m_factor, v_thresh_factor, cm_factor) to PolypState in polyp_neuron.py
2. In lifecycle.py inherit_traits(), also inherit and mutate neural parameter factors using the same bounded log-space mutation
3. In create_lif_params() (polyp_population.py PolypNeuronType), apply the polyp's neural factors to scale base params
4. In add_polyp/restore_polyp, use the polyp's neural factors instead of per-slot Gaussian noise
5. Config flag: lifecycle.enable_neural_heritability (bool, default True)

conditions.description: Lifecycle ON but neural factors are clones (no mutation) — ecological traits still evolve
conditions.name: clone_no_heritability

diagnosis:
Tier 5.25a sham: lifecycle with mutation (PR=2.29) == lifecycle with CLONES/no-mutation (PR=2.29). The PR increase is mathematical inflation from larger population (more channels), not from trait evolution. ALL polyps have IDENTICAL neural computations regardless of their evolved ecological traits.

Root cause: the 11 heritable ecology traits (metabolic_decay, apoptosis_threshold, cyclin_accumulation_rate, etc.) affect WHEN polyps reproduce and die, but have ZERO effect on HOW they compute. tau_m, v_thresh, cm are set from global config defaults with per-slot Gaussian noise (fixed, not heritable).

The fix: map ecological traits to neural parameter scalings via a simple nonlinear mapping. When a polyp reproduces, the child inherits the parent's neural scalings with small mutations. push_all_parameters() already exists for applying changed parameters after mutation.
hypothesis: Adding heritable neural parameter scalings (tau_m_factor, v_thresh_factor, cm_factor) derived from ecological traits and subject to mutation during reproduction will create polyps with genuinely different computational properties. A population with divergent neural parameters will have higher state dimensionality (PR) than a clone population, because different neurons respond to the same input with genuinely different dynamics.

mechanism_under_test.description: Each polyp carries heritable neural scaling factors that multiply the base config values for tau_m, v_thresh, and cm. These factors are inherited from parents with bounded log-space mutation during reproduction.

tau_m_factor.description: Faster metabolism = shorter membrane time constant

mechanism_under_test.name: neural_parameter_heritability

null_hypothesis: Heritable neural parameters do not increase state dimensionality because: (1) the mutation range is too small to create measurable differences, (2) LIF dynamics are insensitive to parameter variation at this scale, or (3) the I/E balance normalizes away individual parameter differences.

outcome_classes.action: Neural parameter heritability creates genuine computational diversity through evolution.
outcome_classes.condition: primary pass AND neurally-heritable beats clone by > 0.3

outcome_classes.action: Neural heritability helps but effect is small; consider stronger mutation.
outcome_classes.condition: PR improves but margin < 0.3 vs clone

outcome_classes.action: Neural parameter variation at this scale is too small to affect LIF dynamics.
outcome_classes.condition: No PR difference between heritable and clone

outcome_classes.action: Parameter bounds need tightening or different neural params.
outcome_classes.condition: Parameter variation destabilizes the network (NaN, extinction, silence)

pass_criteria.primary: heritable PR > clone PR by margin > 0.3 at final timepoint, AND heritable PR > static PR by margin > 0.5, AND neural_factor variance (std across polyps) increases over generations

question: Does making neural parameters (tau_m, v_thresh, cm) heritable and mutable through lifecycle reproduction create genuine computational diversity — polyps evolving genuinely different neural dynamics — beyond the current ecological-only trait heritability?

shams.description: Reproduction ON, ecology traits evolve, but neural_factor mutation is disabled (children inherit exact parent factors). Tests whether neural parameter heritability specifically increases PR beyond ecological trait diversity alone.
shams.name: neural_clones_only

shams.description: Each polyp gets random neural factors at birth (same variance as mutation) but factor NOT inherited from parent. Tests whether heritability (lineage-specific accumulation) is better than random variation.
shams.name: random_neural_assign
